COMPASS_Synoptic_LE_SoilFluxes_2022_CH4

Roberta Peixoto

2026-04-01

Goals of this code:

  1. Reads in raw smart chamber
  2. If no metadatafile, write one out & fields will be: Label the IDs pulled from the label, Plot *fixed so that if there are any issues in the label they are good here Drop column – false as long as label looks right adjust time – would be initially populated by the start time in the OR blank adjust observation length – initially populated by the obs length OR blank notes / comment
  3. Code reads in the meta data and applies it to the data
  1. i.e. drops fluxes that have the drop column TRUE or FLAG this
  2. adjusting times as needed
  1. Now you compute fluxes
  2. Visualize and look for issues
  3. If found, report to meta data file then go back to 3 and reanalyze
  4. Apply algorithmic qaqc and generate flags

Set Up & Load Packages

Pull in Smart Chamber Files from the Raw Data folder

# Create a temporary directory
tmp_dir <- tempdir()

# Get list of files inside the zip
zip_contents <- unzip("Raw Data_LE_2022.zip", list = TRUE)

# Keep only JSON files
json_files <- zip_contents$Name[grepl("json$", zip_contents$Name, ignore.case = TRUE)]

# Extract only the JSON files
unzip(
  "Raw Data_LE_2022.zip",
  files = json_files,
  exdir = tmp_dir
)

# List extracted JSON files (full paths)
file.list <- list.files(
  path = tmp_dir,
  pattern = "json$",
  full.names = TRUE,
  recursive = TRUE
)

head(file.list)

# Read in the data
alldat1 <- lapply(file.list, ffi_read_LIsmartchamber)

# Combine into one data.table
alldat <- data.table::rbindlist(alldat1, fill = TRUE)


## Noticed that the site names were recorded in the file name and not in the flux label for 2022 
## dealing with it in the next chunk

Unfortunatly - we had many problems with labels - we need to include the sites in the Plot_ID

# 1. Table describing the days we have visited each site
site_lookup <- tibble::tribble(
  ~Date,         ~Site,
  "2022-07-07",  "PTR",
  "2022-07-08",  "CRC",
  "2022-07-12",  "CRC",
  "2022-07-14",  "OWC",
  "2022-07-18",  "PTR",
  "2022-07-19",  "PTR",
  "2022-07-20",  "CRC",
  "2022-07-21",  "OWC",
  "2022-08-16",  "CRC",
  "2022-08-17",  "OWC",
  "2022-08-18",  "PTR",
  "2022-08-30",  "MSM",
  "2022-09-16",  "CRC",
  "2022-09-23",  "PTR",
  "2022-09-30",  "OWC",
  "2022-10-05",  "OWC",
  "2022-10-07",  "PTR",
  "2022-10-14",  "CRC",
  "2022-10-21",  "OWC",
  "2022-11-04",  "OWC",
  "2022-11-07",  "CRC",
  "2022-11-08",  "PTR",
  "2022-11-18",  "CRC",
  "2022-12-20",  "OWC"
) %>%
  mutate(Date = as.Date(Date))

# 2. alldat  - date formatting
alldat <- alldat %>%
  mutate(Date = as.Date(TIMESTAMP))%>%
  left_join(site_lookup, by = "Date")

# 4. Fixing label code problems 
alldat <- alldat%>%filter(!label %in% c("UpDemonstration",
                       "WeS22Demonstration")) %>%
  mutate(
    label = label %>%
      str_remove_all("[sSeE]") %>%
      str_replace_all(c(
        "[Ww][tT]"     = "W",
        "[Tt][Rr][a]?" = "TR",  
        "[Uu][Pp]"     = "UP",
        "[Ww][lL]"     = "W"
      )) %>%
      str_replace("(\\d+)([A-Za-z])$", "\\1_\\2") %>%
      str_replace("([A-Z]+)(\\d+)", "\\1_\\2") %>%
      str_c(Site, ., sep = "_")
  )
 
#I have to change labels that were recorded wrong - tracked in the field notes

# OWC 11-4-2022 TR 60 -> use 60c as the others didn't have the swc probe in the soil
# OWC 11-4-2022 W 64 -> 64 is a transition collar, it should be 65
# OWC 09-11-2022 W 71 -> use 71b bc we did not trust first results
# OWC 09-11-2022 W 72 -> 72b forgot the soil probe
# OWC 09-11-2022 TR 61 -> use 61b forgot the soil probe on the 61

Create a unique Plot_ID, check if there is a meta-data file, if not, create a meta-data file

############### only run this first part if you don't already have a meta-data file 
        #because if you do then all the data for each one will have a plot and you won't be able to change start times

#create unique Plot ID for each measurement: 

alldat <- alldat %>% 
  mutate(Date_str = format(as.Date(TIMESTAMP), "%Y_%m_%d"),
         Plot_ID = str_c(Date_str, label, RepNum, sep = "_"))  

#this needs to be Plot ID so that later we ID plot from metadata and not the metadata row 

# Path inside the zip
zip_file <- "Raw Data_LE_2022.zip"
csv_inside_zip <- "Raw Data/COMPASS_SmartChamber_Metadata_2022.csv"

# Make sure the destination folder exists
dir.create("Raw Data", showWarnings = FALSE)

# Extract only the metadata CSV if it doesn't already exist
if (!file.exists(csv_inside_zip)) {
  unzip(
    zip_file,
    files = csv_inside_zip,
    exdir = "."
  )
}

# Define the file path where you want to save the CSV file
file_path <- "Raw Data/COMPASS_SmartChamber_Metadata_2022.csv"

# Check if the CSV file exists
if (file.exists(file_path)) {
  # If it exists, read it back into R
  metadata <- read.csv(file_path)
  print("CSV file exists and has been read into the code.")
} else {
  # If it does not exist, create the CSV file
    #create a metadata file from the Plot IDs 
      # pull timestart timestamps to put in the metadata and obs lengths 
  metadata <- alldat %>%
  group_by(InstrumentSerialNumber, Plot_ID) %>%
  summarise(SN = first(InstrumentSerialNumber), #Roberta => I included this to process more than one licor per time
    Date = format(min(TIMESTAMP), "%Y-%m-%d"),
    Time_start = format(min(TIMESTAMP), "%H:%M:%S"),
    First_Timestamp = min(TIMESTAMP),  # Minimum (earliest) timestamp for each Plot_ID
    Last_Timestamp = max(TIMESTAMP),   # Maximum (latest) timestamp for each Plot_ID
    Total_Time = as.numeric(difftime(max(TIMESTAMP), min(TIMESTAMP), units = "secs")),  # Time difference in seconds
    ) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(Total_Time = pmin(Total_Time, 60),
          drop = FALSE,# Add a 'drop' column with 'FALSE' for every row 
          Notes = "") 
  #mutate(notes = " ")  # Add a 'drop' column with 'FALSE' for every row
  #mutate(Deadband = 5)
  #mutate(Plot = Plot_ID) #think need this because we need to make a plot column
  
  # Write df_unique to CSV
  write.csv(metadata, file = file_path, row.names = FALSE)
  
  print("CSV file does not exist. It has been created.")
}
## [1] "CSV file exists and has been read into the code."
# line 189 :: problems with weird 1017190 second for Total time generated in the metadata for TG 01028 days: 7-7-2022,7-8-2022,7-12-2022,7-14-2022,7-19-2022,-> need to check why later, but I am fixing the timestamp at 120 sec for now

Mark fluxes that were retaken - this is the first issue! Need to get all but last flux to be TRUE for drop - fieldnotes with explanation for flux retaken - usually weird zeros, Steven probe not inserted in the soil, or label mistake

# fluxes that were retaken have similar names with a letter after it 
metadata_filtered <- metadata %>%
  mutate(
    # Base plot ID (everything before the last _number or _number_letter)
    base_id = str_replace(Plot_ID, "_[0-9]+(_[a-z])?$", ""),
    
    # Replicate number (e.g., 32 from 1_Jul_CRC_UP_32 or 1_Jul_CRC_UP_32_b)
    replicate = as.numeric(
      str_extract(Plot_ID, "(?<=_)[0-9]+(?=(_[a-z])?$)")
    ),
    
    # Suffix letter (if present); otherwise empty string
    letter = str_extract(base_id, "(?<=_)[A-Za-z]$"),
    letter = if_else(is.na(letter), "", letter),
    
    # Letter order to define order: "" < a < b < c < d ...
    letter_order = if_else(
      letter == "",
      0L,
      match(letter, letters)  ) ) 


#creating a core ID to compare the retaken obs
metadata_filtered <- metadata_filtered %>%
  mutate(
    # ID for plot not considering retake (remove _a, _b, _c, etc.)
    core_id = str_remove(base_id, "_[A-Za-z]$")
  ) %>%
  group_by(core_id) %>%
  mutate(
    drop = letter_order < max(letter_order, na.rm = TRUE)
  ) %>%
  ungroup()

##Dont need to pull L1 temp data or any other temps like we do for macrophyte because the smartchamber records the temp

Match up the metadata to the rest of the data

#remove any metadata rows that have NAs for the PlotID
metadata_filtered  <- metadata_filtered %>%
  filter(!is.na(Plot_ID)) %>%
  filter(str_detect(Plot_ID, paste(c("OWC", "CRC", "PTR"), collapse = "|"))) 

alldat<-alldat%>% filter(!is.na(label))

#match the altered metadata to the concentration data with fluxfinder ffi_metadata_match 
# this next part was adapted to include SN and process all Licors at the same time, handling overlapping timestamp among >1 licor instruments. 

# matching SN
alldat <- alldat %>%
  rename(SN=InstrumentSerialNumber)%>%
  group_by(SN) %>%
  mutate(
    metadat_row = ffi_metadata_match(
      data_timestamps = TIMESTAMP,
      start_dates     = metadata_filtered$Date[metadata_filtered$SN == first(SN)],
      start_times     = metadata_filtered$Time_start[metadata_filtered$SN == first(SN)],
      obs_lengths     = metadata_filtered$Total_Time[metadata_filtered$SN == first(SN)]
    )
  ) %>%
  ungroup()
## YYYY-MM-DD format failed for start_dates; trying MM/DD/YYYY
## 4 entries had no timestamp matches!
## YYYY-MM-DD format failed for start_dates; trying MM/DD/YYYY
alldat <- alldat %>%
  left_join(
    metadata_filtered %>% mutate(metadat_row = row_number()),
    by = c("SN", "metadat_row", "Plot_ID")
  )

#problems with labels - there was a chamber Offset of 71 instead of 7.1 and chamber volume of 0 from the smart chamber

alldat <- alldat %>%
  mutate(
    # Fix the Offset bug: 71 -> 7.1 and recalculating volume based on different Offsets
    Offset_cm = if_else(Offset == 71, 7.1, Offset),

    # Fix chamber volume when it is zero
    ChamVolume_fixed_cm3 = if_else(
      ChamVolume == 0,
      4244.1,
      ChamVolume
    ),

    # Recalculate collar volume (cm3)
    CollarVolume_cm3 = Area * Offset_cm,

    # Recalculate total volume (cm3)
    TotalVolume_fixed_cm3 =
      ChamVolume_fixed_cm3 +
      IrgaVolume +
      CollarVolume_cm3,
  )

fixing some Deadbands Revisit here, it is not working to fix the dead band

# Table of plots with custom deadband adjustments
deadband_overrides <- tibble::tibble(
  Plot_ID = c(
    "2022_08_16_CRC_W_43_1",
    "2022_08_17_OWC_TR_64_1",
    "2022_07_19_PTR_UP_1_2",
    "2022_07_19_PTR_TR_15Mid_1",
     "2022_07_19_PTR_UP_8Mid2_2",
    "2022_11_08_PTR_W_luci_vg2_1",
    "2022_10_05_OWC_W_66_2",
    "2022_10_05_OWC_W_65_1",
    "2022_08_17_OWC_W_68_c_1",
    "2022_07_08_CRC_UP_27_1",
    "2022_07_07_PTR_UP_1_1",
    "2022_07_21_OWC_TR_63_2",
    "2022_07_20_CRC_W_46_b_2",
    "2022_07_19_PTR_TR_16Mid2_2",
    "2022_09_23_PTR_TR_11_1",
    "2022_07_20_CRC_W_45_1",
    "2022_07_19_PTR_UP_1Mid_2",
    "2022_08_16_CRC_W_41_2",
    "2022_08_17_OWC_W_66_2",
    "2022_10_14_CRC_W_43_1",
    "2022_07_08_CRC_TR_35_1",
    "2022_07_12_CRC_TR_37_2",
    "2022_07_07_PTR_W_24_1",
    "2022_07_08_CRC_W_41_1",
    "2022_07_20_CRC_TR_37_1",
    "2022_07_18_PTR_UP_20_c_2",
    "2022_07_20_CRC_UP_26_1",
    "2022_07_20_CRC_UP_29_2",
    "2022_07_08_CRC_TR_37_2",

    "2022_07_18_PTR_UP_01_b_2",
    "2022_07_08_CRC_W_43_2",
    "2022_07_08_CRC_W_43_1",
    "2022_10_05_OWC_W_66_1", #GOOD LINEAR FLAT LINE - NO - FLUX 
     "2022_09_30_OWC_W_65_1",
    "2022_07_20_CRC_UP_29_2",
    "2022_07_07_PTR_W_22_1",
    "2022_07_20_CRC_TR_37_1",
    "2022_07_19_PTR_TR_16Mid_1",
    "2022_07_07_PTR_UP_2_2",
    "2022_08_18_PTR_UP_01_b_2",
    "2022_07_20_CRC_UP_26_1",
    "2022_07_18_PTR_TR_10_2",
    "2022_07_20_CRC_UP_29_2",
    "2022_07_19_PTR_TR_16Mid2_2",
    "2022_11_08_PTR_W_luci_NoVg2_1",
    "2022_11_08_PTR_W_luci_NoVg1_2",
    "2022_09_30_OWC_W_66_2"
    ),
  
  
  DeadBand_override = c(
    26,
    9,
    12,
    18,
    10,
    24,
    18,
    13,
    10,
    10,
    11,
    10,

    9,

    10,
    15,
    10,
    11,
    11,
    13,
    10,
    10,
    13,
    9,
    14,
    12
  )
)

# Default deadband
alldat <- alldat %>%
  mutate(DeadBand_used = DeadBand) 

# Apply per-plot overrides
alldat <- alldat %>%
  left_join(deadband_overrides, by = "Plot_ID") %>%
  mutate(
    DeadBand_used = if_else(
      !is.na(DeadBand_override),
      DeadBand_override,
      DeadBand_used
    )
  ) %>%
  select(-DeadBand_override)
## Warning in left_join(., deadband_overrides, by = "Plot_ID"): Detected an unexpected many-to-many relationship between `x` and `y`.
## ℹ Row 13802 of `x` matches multiple rows in `y`.
## ℹ Row 10 of `y` matches multiple rows in `x`.
## ℹ If a many-to-many relationship is expected, set `relationship =
##   "many-to-many"` to silence this warning.

Change the units from ppb to nmol

#change the units
alldat$CH4_nmol <- ffi_ppb_to_nmol(alldat$ch4, 
                                volume = (alldat$TotalVolume_fixed_cm3 / 1000000), # have to divide to get to m3
                                temp = alldat$chamber_t)    # degrees C
## Assuming atm = 101325 Pa
## Using R = 8.31446261815324 m3 Pa K-1 mol-1
#> Assuming atm = 101325 Pa
#> Using R = 8.31446261815324 m3 Pa K-1 mol-1

alldat$CH4_nmol_m2 <- alldat$CH4_nmol / 0.0318 #adjust for the area

Calculate fluxes

#need to create a new line that would say only calculate the flux if the drop column is false
alldat_dropped<-alldat%>%filter(drop!=TRUE)

#selecting the base id to include in the flux df later
plot_base_lookup <- alldat %>%
  select(Plot_ID, base_id)%>% 
  filter(!is.na(base_id)) %>%   
  distinct(Plot_ID, .keep_all = TRUE)


#calculate fluxes for all other sites 
fluxes <- ffi_compute_fluxes(alldat_dropped,
                             group_column = "Plot_ID", 
                             time_column = "TIMESTAMP", 
                             gas_column = "CH4_nmol_m2", 
                             dead_band = "DeadBand_used")
head(fluxes)
#including the unique ID 
fluxes <- fluxes %>%
  left_join(plot_base_lookup, by = "Plot_ID")

##Create a dataframe with averaged soil probe data per Plot and add to flux data
soildat <- alldat %>% 
    group_by(Plot_ID) %>%
  dplyr::summarize(
     soilp_c = mean(soilp_c, na.rm=TRUE),
     soilp_m = mean(soilp_m, na.rm=TRUE),
     soilp_t = mean(soilp_t, na.rm=TRUE))

alldf <- left_join(fluxes, soildat, by = "Plot_ID")

#reading the list of negative CO2 flux 
drop_neg_CO2 <- readRDS("drop_neg_CO2_2022.rds")%>%
  dplyr::select(Plot_ID,drop_CO2)

alldf <- alldf %>%
  left_join(drop_neg_CO2, by = "Plot_ID")%>%
  mutate(drop_CO2 = coalesce(drop_CO2, FALSE))


allflux <-alldf%>%filter(drop_CO2!=TRUE)

Visualize fluxes and sort by r2

#take a look at the fluxes we calculated with R2 
ggplot(allflux, aes(Plot_ID, lin_flux.estimate, color = lin_r.squared)) +
  geom_point(size=4) + theme_classic() + ylim(-50,2000) + 
  geom_linerange(aes(ymin = lin_flux.estimate- lin_flux.std.error,
                   ymax = lin_flux.estimate+ lin_flux.std.error)) +
  scale_color_gradientn(colours = c("darkred",  "red", "white","lightblue", "darkblue"),
                        values = c(0, 0.5, 0.6, 0.7, 1)) +
  ylab("CH4 flux (nmol/m2/s)")

#sort fluxes by R2 
fluxes_good <- as.data.frame(subset(allflux, lin_r.squared >= .90, select = Plot_ID:TIMESTAMP_max))
fluxes_lowr2 <- as.data.frame(subset(allflux, lin_r.squared < .90 & lin_flux.estimate < 1, select = Plot_ID:TIMESTAMP_max))
fluxes_weird <- as.data.frame(subset(allflux, lin_r.squared < .90 & lin_flux.estimate > 1, select = Plot_ID:TIMESTAMP_max))

#find fluxes that represent the first and fourth quartile of the data 
low <- quantile(allflux$lin_flux.estimate, 0.05, na.rm=TRUE) 
high <- quantile(allflux$lin_flux.estimate, 0.95, na.rm=TRUE) 

fluxes_low <- as.data.frame(subset(allflux, lin_flux.estimate <= low, select = Plot_ID:TIMESTAMP_max))
fluxes_high <- as.data.frame(subset(allflux, lin_flux.estimate >= high, select = Plot_ID:TIMESTAMP_max))

tails <- rbind(fluxes_low, fluxes_high)

check the fluxes for which I changed the start time due to lags or ebullition (recorded in notes of metadata)

#### If something is in a V shape should we take the second part to make a positive linear curve?

# Subset data and create relative time (seconds since flux start)
r_all <- alldat %>%
  filter(Plot_ID %in% deadband_overrides$Plot_ID) %>%
  group_by(Plot_ID) %>%
  mutate(
    time_sec = as.numeric(
      difftime(TIMESTAMP, min(TIMESTAMP), units = "secs")
    )
  ) %>%
  ungroup()


# Random sample of plots for visualization
set.seed(123)

plots_sample <- sample(
  unique(r_all$Plot_ID),
  5,
  replace = FALSE
)

r_plot <- r_all %>%
  filter(Plot_ID %in% plots_sample)

# Plot
ggplot(
  r_plot,
  aes(
    x = TIMESTAMP,
    y = ch4,
    color = time_sec >= DeadBand_used
  )
) +
  geom_point(size = 1.8) +
  facet_wrap(~ Plot_ID, scales = "free", ncol = 2) +
  scale_color_manual(
    values = c("grey60", "red"),
    labels = c("Discarded (deadband)", "Used after deadband")
  ) +
  theme_classic() +
  ylab(expression(paste(CH[4], " (ppb)"))) +
  xlab("Time")

Remove any fluxes that do not fit criteria

##                    Plot_ID           TIMESTAMP  HM81_AIC HM81_flux.estimate
## 1   2022_07_07_PTR_TR_10_1 2022-07-07 13:19:48  80.35382          0.1937105
## 2   2022_07_07_PTR_TR_10_2 2022-07-07 13:22:49 107.87067         -0.3529438
## 3 2022_07_07_PTR_TR_11_b_1 2022-07-07 13:46:59        NA                 NA
## 4 2022_07_07_PTR_TR_11_b_2 2022-07-07 13:49:59        NA                 NA
## 5   2022_07_07_PTR_TR_12_1 2022-07-07 13:29:54 134.20679         -0.6536427
## 6   2022_07_07_PTR_TR_12_2 2022-07-07 13:32:54 120.28252         -0.5976115
##   HM81_p.value HM81_r.squared HM81_RMSE  lin_AIC lin_flux.estimate
## 1 1.755775e-12      0.7828874 0.7208524  86.8641         0.1278211
## 2 3.083243e-14      0.8297816 1.0679885 112.7757        -0.2231831
## 3           NA             NA        NA 117.3050        -1.7469213
## 4           NA             NA        NA 110.0656        -1.3772775
## 5 3.652531e-24      0.9572272 1.5558272 121.5809        -0.7124048
## 6 5.791799e-28      0.9748173 1.2751823 116.1455        -0.7638879
##   lin_flux.std.error lin_int.estimate lin_int.std.error  lin_p.value
## 1         0.01324047         17926.36         0.5719515 3.882714e-11
## 2         0.01917186         17649.05         0.8281709 3.159353e-13
## 3         0.02045339         18157.73         0.8835294 2.652683e-40
## 4         0.01844380         18221.24         0.7967211 2.183523e-38
## 5         0.02174171         17386.07         0.9391815 9.437158e-27
